assertions.title: Starting solo creates a playable live round

steps.action: Open the app on a mobile viewport and inspect the solo pre-start state
steps.expected: Solo setup is visible before play with disabled word entry and a start CTA
steps.observed: Pre-start home/solo surface showed `Dictionary ready`, timer `01:00`, disabled word entry, and `Start solo round`.

steps.expected: Setup state hides and a live solo round renders
steps.observed: Setup controls were replaced by the live solo surface with a populated 4x4 board and play controls only.

steps.action: Inspect the active round immediately after start/restart
steps.expected: Countdown begins only after the board is visibly ready, word entry is enabled, and controls remain reachable
steps.observed: Restart timeline showed the board fully populated and `Board ready. Start finding words.` with timer still at `01:00` for the first 750ms samples; timer dropped to `00:59` only after the ready state was visible. Word entry was enabled and `Add word`, `Reveal words`, `Restart round`, and `Show entered words` remained reachable on mobile.

evidence.network: No relevant API/network activity beyond initial local page/assets fetch; no backend dependency observed for this assertion.

assertions.reasoning: The tested mobile flow satisfied the contract language: starting solo hid setup UI, rendered a fully populated board, kept the timer at its full starting value until the ready state was already visible, enabled entry, and kept primary play controls reachable without overflow.

summary: Retest passed: VAL-SOLO-003 now presents a playable mobile live round with visible ready state before countdown progression and reachable play controls.
